mechanism_narrative: A persistently elevated FEDFUNDS level above its trailing 12-month average signals restrictive monetary policy that raises the discount rate on equity cash flows, systematically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days relative to periods of low or falling rates.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.33% in Aug-2025 vintage, well above 2020-2021 near-zero regime
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns stays below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window, or high-FEDFUNDS periods produce forward returns statistically indistinguishable from low-FEDFUNDS periods (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points relative to its trailing 12-month low (Sahm-rule-style trigger) signals an emerging recessionary labor shock, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations deteriorate.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp above its trailing 12-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.3% in Aug-2025 vintage, near multi-year range
failure_condition: Triggered instances show positive 60d forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or the signal fails to outperform an unconditional baseline by a statistically significant margin (p>0.05) over the 2005-2024 sample.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth decelerates while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, the joint condition of weakening consumer demand and tight monetary policy compounds to produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days, exceeding the effect of either signal alone because tight policy removes the offset that easing would otherwise provide to slowing demand.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth turns negative while FEDFUNDS > 4%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 726,283M in Jul-2025 vintage with FEDFUNDS at 4.33%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with decelerating consumption
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances do not produce more negative average forward returns than the RSAFS-only or FEDFUNDS-only single-factor triggers, or interaction coefficient in a regression with both main effects is not statistically significant (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and improving corporate revenue momentum, translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as short-horizon sentiment tracks real-side production strength.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised value 101.894 vs vintage 103.9867 in Jul-2025, showing recent industrial output level
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive INDPRO 3-month trend are not significantly different from returns following negative trend (p>0.10) across the full 2005-2024 sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime, a decline in HOUST signals housing-sector stress that precedes broader credit and consumption contraction, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days; the same HOUST decline in an easing regime is absorbed by falling rates and does not reliably predict negative returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 10% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS is above its 24-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1432K units (revised) in Jul-2025 vintage under FEDFUNDS 4.33%
failure_condition: The negative-return relationship for HOUST declines disappears or reverses when tested only within tightening-regime subsamples, or the same HOUST decline in easing regimes produces equally negative returns, disproving regime dependence.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in PAYEMS exceeding 100,000 jobs signals an abrupt labor market deterioration that precedes downward revisions to earnings forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised value 158,472K vs vintage 159,540K in Aug-2025, indicating a downward revision of over 1,000K
failure_condition: Triggered PAYEMS decline events show forward 20d returns that are positive in more than 55% of instances, or PAYEMS-based signal Rank IC stays below 0.02 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS is simultaneously near cycle highs, the combination signals policy is unlikely to pivot dovish soon, compounding valuation pressure and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days beyond what either inflation or rate levels predict independently.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 323.291 (revised) with FEDFUNDS at 4.33% in Aug-2025 vintage
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL YoY change and FEDFUNDS level is not statistically significant in a regression predicting 5d forward returns (p>0.10), or joint-trigger returns do not differ from single-factor trigger returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in RSAFS reflecting steady consumer spending growth signals resilient aggregate demand, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as corporate revenue expectations remain anchored to consumption strength.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised value 727,727M vs vintage 726,283M in Jul-2025, indicating continued growth
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS 6-month trend periods do not produce statistically higher 60d forward returns than negative-trend periods (p>0.10) across the 2005-2024 sample, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 in more than half of rolling 3-year windows.
